questions.question: 1 (a) The enthalpy change of atomisation of iodine (+107 kJ mol –1 ) is given by the equation (1) A ½I 2 (g) → I(g) B ½I 2 (s) → I(g) C I 2 (g) → 2I(g) D I 2 (s) → 2I(g) (b) Use the information in the cycle to calculate the electron affinity of iodine. (1) A –298 kJ mol –1 B –242 kJ mol –1 C
questions.text: 1 (a) The enthalpy change of atomisation of iodine (+107 kJ mol –1 ) is given by the equation (1) A ½I 2 (g) → I(g) B ½I 2 (s) → I(g) C I 2 (g) → 2I(g) D I 2 (s) → 2I(g) (b) Use the information in the cycle to calculate the electron affinity of iodine. (1) A –298 kJ mol –1 B –242 kJ mol –1 C

parts.text: 1 (a) The enthalpy change of atomisation of iodine (+107 kJ mol –1 ) is given by the equation (1) A ½I 2 (g) → I(g) B ½I 2 (s) → I(g) C I 2 (g) → 2I(g) D I 2 (s) → 2I(g) (b) Use the information in the cycle to calculate the electron affinity of iodine. (1) A –298 kJ mol –1 B –242 kJ mol –1 C
parts.answer: The only correct answer is B ( ½ I (s) → I (g) ) A is incorrect because atomisation of an element is from its standard state and iodine is a solid C is incorrect because atomisation produces 1 mole of atoms and requires solid iodine D is incorrect because atomisation produces 1 mole of atoms (1) Question Number Correct Answer Mark

The only corre ct answer is A ( ‒ 298 kJ mol ‒ ) B is incorrect because this value has had 28 added to ‒ 270 rather than subtracted from it C is incorrect because first electron af finity values are always exothermic D is incorrect because first electr on affinity values are always ex othermic and the wrong sign has been used for the enthalpy change of hydration (1) Question Number Correct Answer Mark The only correct answer is B ( ‒ 1650 kJ mol ‒ ) A is incorrect because this uses the wrong sign for the enthalpy change of solution C is not correct because this uses only one mole of chloride ions D is not correct because this does not ch ange the sign of the lattice enthalpy (1) Question Number Correct Answer Mark

The only corre ct answer is A (the mole fraction of carbon dioxide) B is incorrect because the equilibrium will move to the left hand side so this will decrease C is not correct because the rate of both reactions will decrease at lower temperature D is incorrect because the equilibrium will move to the left hand side so this will decrease (1) Question Number Correct Answer Mark

parts.text: 3 (b) The rate equation for the reaction between propanone and iodine in acidic conditions is rate = k[H + ][CH 3 COCH 3 ] The r eaction was carried out at two different pH values, all other conditions remaining unchanged. In the first reaction pH = 2.0 In the second reaction the rate was found to be 1/3 of the original value. What was the pH of the second reaction, to 1 decimal place?
parts.answer: The only correct answer is C (0.474) A is incorrect because this answer divides the mole fraction of carbon dioxide by 2 B is incorrect because this answer divides the mole fraction of carbon monoxide by 2 D is incorrect because this is the partial pressure of carbon monoxide (1) Question Number Correct Answer Mark The only correct answer is A (dm mol ‒ ) B is incorrect because the units of concentratio n should be raised to the power of -3 not -2 C is incorrect because the units should be the recipr ocal of concentration raised to the power of -3 not -2 D is incorrect because the units should be the recipr ocal of concentration raised to the power of -3 not -2 (1) Question Number Correct Answer Mark The only correct answer is D (phenolphthalein) A is incorrect because the indicator needs a range contained between pH 8 and pH 11 B is incorrect because the indicator needs a range contained between pH 8 and pH 11 C is incorrect because the indicator needs a range contained between pH 8 and pH 11 (1) Question Number Correct Answer Mark The only correct answer is A (the dissociation of water is endothermic, so the concentration of hydrogen ions is higher at 100 o C than it is at 25 o C) B is incorrect because at higher temper atures more hydrogen ions are present C is incorrect because the dissociation of water is endothermic D is incorrect because the dissoci ation of water is endothermic (1) Question Number Correct Answer Mark The only correct answer is C (C H O ) A is incorrect because there are 16 carbon atoms in ketoprofen B is incorrect because this answer has one hydrogen too few D is incorrect because this answer assumes there is 1 hydrogen on each carbon in the benzene rings (1) Question Number Correct Answer Mark The only correct answer is C (3) A is incorrect because there are three chiral centres B is incorrect because ther e are three chiral centres D is incorrect because ther e are three chiral centres (1) Question Number An answer that makes reference to the following points:  there are only 4 particles in the rate equation and 12 in the equation for the reaction OR collisions with more than 2 particles are unlikely Accept the number of particles in the rate equation does not match the equation for the reaction Accept the chances of collisions of 3 / 4 / many particles is unlikely Do not accept other numbers of particles Accept comparison of numbers of particles of individual ions in the equation of the reaction and in the rate equation / order of reaction, e.g. 5 [Br ‒ ] in the equation but only 1 in the rate equation ALLOW molecules / ions / species / concentrations instead of particles ALLOW TE for comparison on (a)(i) and (a)(ii) (1) (Total for Question 18 = 8 marks) Number Additional Guidance Mark An answer that makes reference to the following points: Step 1  lone pair of electrons on C of C ≡ N  curly arrow from anywhere on the C of C ≡ N to C in propanal including the charge  curly arrow from C=O bond to or just beyond O  dipole on C=O Step 2  lone pair on O in intermediate Step 1 or Step 2  curly arrow from the O (or minus charge) of intermediate to H of H-C ≡ N  curly arrow from H-C bond to C of H-C ≡ N All 7 points scores 4 marks 5 or 6 points scores 3 marks 3 or 4 points scores 2 marks 2 points scores 1 mark Ignore formula of products even if incorrect Ignore all dipoles on HCN Penalise dipoles on C-O in the intermediate (4)  +  - Structure may be skeletal or displayed or a mixture, as long as it is clear. Allow, for example, a displayed formula with condensed CH . Ignore connectivity of -OH (2) (Total for Question 20 = 20 marks) Number Additional Guidance Mark  calculates moles of acid present in the mixture  calculates moles of ester and water present in the mixture  calculates moles of ethanol present in the mixture  expression for K c and final answer (1) (1) (1) (1) Example calculation mol of acid = mol of NaOH = 34.8 x 2.50 = 0.087 (mol) mol of ester = mol of water = 0.2 – 0.087 = 0.113(mol) mol of ethanol = 0.150 – 0.113 = 0.037 If the expression for Kc is incorrect, e.g. no water, allow TE on M1-3 for example not calculating moles of water as well as ester K c = 0.113/V x 0.113/V = 3.9668 / 4.0 (no units) 0.087/V x 0.037/V OR K c = [ CH COOC H ][H O] = 3.9668 / 4.0 and statement that [CH COOH][C H OH] volumes cancel Do not penalise lack of square brackets in equilibrium expression Assumption that 0.087 is moles of acid used gives moles ethanol = 0.063 moles ester = water = 0.087 Kc = 1.0632 scores max (3) Calculation of acid moles at equilibrium larger than acid moles at the start can score M4 only If no other mark is scored Award (1) for calculation of 0.087(mol) however it is used, Ignore SF (4) Number Additional Guidance Mark An answer that makes reference to the following points:  same type of / similar bonds being broken and made  same number of each type of bond being broken and made (1) (1) Allow O-H and C-O bonds being broken and made Allow the same bond being broken and made Allow C-OH Ignore C-O-H and COH Ignore CO without the bond shown Award 2 marks for a complete list of the bonds being broken and made e.g. Bonds broken and ma
